overview.what: A simulation of a distributed caching layer that uses a Consistent Hashing ring to route keys to specific cache nodes. It demonstrates how systems like Memcached or DynamoDB minimize data reshuffling when nodes leave or join the cluster.
overview.how_it_works: Unlike standard modulo hashing (which reassigns all keys when the number of nodes changes), consistent hashing maps both nodes and keys to a large circular range. Moving a node only affects k/n keys, significantly reducing cache misses during scaling events.
what_you_learn: Visualizing key distribution across a ring
Using virtual nodes to improve balance
